fields.label: Reading
fields.value: I Came Upon a Lighthouse — partially read

fields.label: Status
fields.value: None yet obtained — all in planning

lead:
This page documents planned learning — certifications not yet obtained, tools not yet mastered, and courses in progress. **Nothing here represents a current skill or finished item.** This is an honest future agenda.

The overarching career direction is **Computer Networking, Cloud Computing, and Cybersecurity** — and the certification plan reflects this. The priority sequence targets networking fundamentals first (CompTIA Network+, CCNA), then security (Security+, CEH), then cloud (AWS, OCI, Azure).

content.content:
**CS50 Introduction to Cybersecurity (Harvard)** — Started on edX / cs50.harvard.edu. Covers cybersecurity fundamentals: threats, vulnerabilities, defensive techniques, cryptography basics, web security. Status: started, not yet completed. Remaining modules and final project still to be done.

**Vim / Neovim** — Not fully started. Learning through vimtutor (built into Vim). Target: keyboard-driven terminal editing that requires no GUI. Worth learning for serious terminal work where LibreWolf or VS Code aren't available. Neovim with LazyVim config is already the editor in the [[linux-setup|Omarchy setup]], but deeper proficiency is still being developed.

content.title: Reading
content.content: Partially reading I Came Upon a Lighthouse by Shantanu Naidu — a personal memoir about an intergenerational friendship and mentorship. Not a business book. About human connection and life lessons. Need to finish the remaining chapters.

content.title: No industry certifications obtained yet
content.content: As of April 2026, no formal industry certifications have been obtained. The Linux Fundamentals course certificate is a basic course cert. CS50 Cybersecurity is started but not completed. Everything listed below is planned with no set timeline.

rows.Notes: Good foundation before Security+. Networking fundamentals.
Gold standard for networking roles in India. High priority.
Most recognised first cert in cybersecurity. After Network+.
EC-Council — offensive security focus. After Security+.
Oracle Cloud — relevant given existing OCI homelab usage.

content.content:
**Obsidian (advanced features)** — Currently using Obsidian as a personal wiki host but not using it beyond basic note-taking. Features to learn: Dataview plugin (query notes like a database), Canvas (visual mind maps), graph filters, templates, tag hierarchies, backlink navigation, and core plugin configuration. Self-study via official docs and community guides.

**Hermes Agent (Nous Research)** — A self-improving autonomous AI agent (Feb 2026, 123,000+ GitHub stars, MIT license). Runs across 15+ platforms from a single install. Natural language cron jobs, model-agnostic, closed learning loop, GEPA self-improvement. Status: on hold indefinitely. The current Oracle Cloud VM (1 OCPU, 1 GB RAM) is too limited — Hermes needs 24 GB+ RAM. Blocked until the [[homelab|ARM Ampere situation]] resolves.

The certification plan prioritises **CCNA and Security+** as the two highest-value items for the target career in networking and cloud. Both are recognised hiring signals in India — particularly CCNA for network engineering roles and Security+ for security-adjacent roles.

The approach throughout is to build real things alongside studying theory. Each certification target connects to something already built: the [[privacy-stack|Privacy Stack]] demonstrates the practical networking knowledge that Network+ covers in theory; the [[homelab|homelab architecture]] demonstrates the cloud concepts that AWS/OCI certs formalise.

No timeline is set for any certification. The honest status is: none started, all planned.
None of these are current skills or finished. These are honest future agenda items.
content.author: Learning Plans document, April 2026

editHistory.summary: Added CS50 status, Hermes constraint note, cert sequence clarified
